title: Pilot 001: Architecture Review Board
productQuestion: Can VALOUR turn a high-pressure architecture leadership moment into a clearer decision conversation?
scenario: A solution architect is preparing for an architecture review board under delivery pressure. The architect needs to present a cloud integration design clearly, avoid over-explaining, and make the decision, trade-off, and risk position understandable to the review board.

stages.label: Intake
stages.summary: Captures the user's role, architecture leadership challenge, review board context, starting confidence, desired outcome, main concern, and selected scenario.

stages.label: Scenario Selection
stages.summary: Confirms Architecture Review Board as the correct VALOUR scenario because the user must convert technical judgement into decision language under challenge.

stages.label: Preparation
stages.summary: Defines audience, stakeholders, decision required, context, risk, likely pushback, response options, opening frame, questions, and closing statement.

stages.label: Rehearsal
stages.summary: Runs simulated board challenge questions and shows how VALOUR improves broad or defensive answers into concise decision-oriented responses.

stages.label: Language Refinement
stages.summary: Converts technical, uncertain, or defensive answers into executive, empathetic, boundary-setting, and decision-oriented language.

stages.label: After-Action Review
stages.summary: Records what happened, what worked, what did not, where clarity improved, where composure was tested, feedback received, and ending confidence.

stages.label: Pattern Report
stages.summary: Identifies the leadership pattern, primary strength, primary risk, best and hardest scenario types, development focus, and coaching note.

stages.label: Evidence Record
stages.summary: Summarises product evidence, confidence shift, observed improvement, product learning, remaining weakness, recommendation, and repeat decision.
